text: Stockpiling will allow us to safeguard against crop failure in the near future whilst contributing to our clan's growth.
A wonderful idea; grand festivities will help ease the minds of the people, if only for a while.
They are right to be angry. We have lost sight of traditional ways and values through excessive modernisation. We must temper our rate of modernisation.
Outrage! Do they not see how the people benefit from exposure to other cultures? These criminals must be brought to justice!
We welcome the opportunity to engage in material and cultural exchange with the nanban. We will build them a trading post.
In these trying times, all assistance is welcome. Dispatch an envoy to Miidera at once.

My lord, one of our valiant warriors has proved his mettle in securing this victory for the clan. It is our belief that his talents are wasted in the rank-and-file and that he will best serve the clan leading our forces in battle.
Can Amaterasu be warning us of troubled times to come? We must remain cautious.
Seeking to further the clan's mastery of the arts our retainers request guidance as to where they should focus their efforts.
In these trying times, all assistance is welcome. Dispatch an envoy to Enryakuji at once.
We currently have no need for their services, they will have to seek their fortune elsewhere.
We can exploit these masterless warriors to our own military ends. We will enlist them at once.

My lord, the son of one of your most loyal subjects - a fine warrior, I am told -  desires to prove his worth to you and the clan. As such, he requests the opportunity to lead your glorious forces in battle.
Medical science benefits the lives of both civilians and the military alike. We should focus on western medicine.
We cannot afford to make such an investment at this time. We must rely on our existing resources to equip the troops.
We may have embraced Christianity, but we cannot allow such an affront towards those who practice the old beliefs. The nanban will answer for their behaviour.
Heavy drinking is a base and shameful practice. All offenders will incur a fine and anyone behaving disgracefully will lose their head.
Hachiman favours our war efforts and grants our warriors the blessings of Heaven!
This is war, and there can be only one victor. We must maintain our superiority even if that means exploiting our enemy's misfortunes.
As you sit in a secluded shrine to Amaterasu, contemplating matters of Heaven and Earth, a three-legged crow perches upon a branch before you.
A flight of doves has been seen circling the capital, my lord. Hachiman's flock brings divine blessings upon our clan!
Nonsense! The Imperialists hold no sway over our people, we will deal their discontent in due course.
We will bolster our defensive capabilities for the protection of our lands and our people.
We have no need to further invest in the support of our monks for the time being. We decline the Ikko-Ikki's offer.
We need time to consider the options. Inform them that - for the time being at least - we decline.
We must assure the future of our clan, and this man has proved his dedication to our cause. We will adopt him as heir.
The Shogunate's desire for order has resulted in their offer to bestow special authority upon our metsuke, as long as we are willing to offer tribute.
The truth regarding Taira treachery must be known to all. We will sponsor this man.
In this time of war, we need as many able warriors in our ranks as possible; they will best serve the clan by fighting our enemies.
A marvellous idea! See to it that each shirabyoshi is furnished with a copy.
We have neither the time nor the money to squander on such superstitious frivolities.
We have no need to engage in trade with the nanban at this time. Perhaps at a later time?
We have no need to engage in trade with the nanban at this time. Perhaps at a later time?
Procure as you deem necessary; a shirabyoshi relies greatly on her repertoire, and there are important lessons to be learned from history.
The Hattori are indeed masters of ninjutsu; their knowledge of the art will help us to improve our own subterfuge capabilities. We accept their offer.
My lord, a representative of the Hattori has approached us with a proposition. In return for a small tribute, they will be willing to share some of their expert knowledge of ninjutsu with us.
William Willis, a British physician residing in Japan, seeks temporary employment and is offering his services as medical officer.
Insolence will not be tolerated. I will have his head on a spike!
This man’s eagerness to serve is admirable, but we do not require his services for the time being.
We will not stoop to persecuting people for holding beliefs different from our own. Were we not once Buddhist ourselves?
We need not invest further in the support of our metsuke for the time being. We must decline the shogun's offer.
Deference will be shown to the great kami of the mountains; see to it that no expense is spared in the preparation of the appropriate rites and festivities.

We have been approached by the French. Staunch supporters of the Shogun, they have offered to establish a military mission here to improve our military potential and improve trade relations.\\n\\nBy accepting we forego the opportunity to establish a military mission with other western powers.
My lord! The monks speak of a rumbling deep beneath the mountains and believe this to be a sign sent by Oyamatsumi. They fear the wrath of the earth and beseech you to appease the mountain-god.
Here is a way to improve international relations and cultural exchange at the same time. We await word of their travels with baited breath!

Public order in this province is low, and Imperialist insurgents have been attempting to capitalise on the situation in order to promote anti-Shogunate dissent. It could be wise to invest in additional policing.
The clan stands to benefit financially from these people. We will allow them to prosper, for now.
In spite of their low social status, traders and merchants have gained substantial influence of late as our clan's efforts in this sphere have increased.
We will arrange a clan-wide chinka-sai at once; see to it that no expense is spared in the preparation of the rites and the festivities.
We will arrange shiomatsuri in all provinces that sit upon the Owatatsumi's waters. See to it that no expense is spared in the preparations of the rites and the festivities.
A scholar seeks your endorsement, hoping to chronicle the events of the Heiji Rebellion, when Lord Kiyomori valiantly defended the cloistered Emperor against the ambitions of Minamoto warmongers.
We should work towards improving our armies.
Our just acts have brought the wrath of the Minamoto down upon us. Once the truth is known the loyalty of our people will be assured. We will sponsor his work.

Strength in numbers and troop variety will ensure that we can rise to any challenge. Our navy can provide invaluable support to our armies, landing troops behind enemy lines and bombarding from afar. We should decide where to focus our military might.
My lord! The monks speak of a gathering of great turtles upon our shores and believe this to be a sign sent by Owatatsumi. They fear ominous tides and beseech you to honour the sea-god.
We need all the help we can get in this conflict. Pay the man, may he show us his artillery skills.
Although deserving of death, his unscrupulous talents could be put to use against our enemies. Inform him that he will serve us or die… slowly.
Justice must be served. The offender will be forcibly removed from the land and must return it to its rightful owner. If he resists, he will be executed.
The Enryakuji monks are valued friends. We will help fund their struggle.
We will arrange a clan-wide Fujin-sai at once; see to it that no expense is spared in the preparation of the appropriate rites and festivities.
We cannot be held responsible for the consequences of foreign indiscretion, regardless of the severity.

Once more, the British have offered to establish a military mission here to improve our military potential and improve trade relations. Should we decline again, I fear that they will not offer again.\\n\\nBy accepting we forego the opportunity to establish a military mission with other western powers.
There is no need for religious persecution; spiritual harmony can be achieved through discourse and understanding.
We need time to consider the options. Inform them that - for the time being at least - we decline.
We can exploit these masterless warriors to our own military ends. We will enlist them at once.

An increasing amount of wako activity is compromising our trade with China. As we are operating without the approval of the Ming Emperor, we cannot expect China to take any steps towards resolving this issue.
We can ill-afford to waste precious funds when so much is at stake.
Before looking to trade opportunities in far-away lands, we must concentrate on refining its practice within Japan.
We have neither the time nor the money to squander on such superstitious frivolities.
We cannot allow the concerns of a few locals to affect our good relations with the western powers. Take no action.
We haven't the resources to spare given the more pressing matters of clan development and warfare at hand.
Slavery is barbaric! We cannot stand idly by whilst such inhumanity persists on our shores.

The amount of wako activity in the vicinity of Tsushima island has increased dramatically. These acts of piracy have compromised our prolific trade links with Korea and should be stopped at the first opportunity.
Western learning might benefit us greatly, even if we disapprove of the gaijin's crude and self-serving ways.
Two crewmen from HMS Icarus, a British vessel moored at one of our trading ports, have been murdered by local anti-foreigner activists. The British are demanding we pay damages.
What good is military might without the infrastructure to support and exploit it? We will focus on administrative development.
We cannot accept. Convey our regrets to the United States ambassador.
In these trying times, all assistance is welcome. Dispatch an envoy to Kofukuji at once.
This man has done no more than carry out his duties; we expect no less from our subjects.
In these trying times, all assistance is welcome. Dispatch an envoy to Miidera at once.
Agreed. See to it that none but those serving in the military may bear arms.
The Imperial court is preparing for the new year's felicitations in honour of His Imperial Majesty. They seek tribute from his true and deferent lords so that due respect can be paid.
Foolishness! To abide such a request would be to empower our rivals! We will hear no more about it.
We will benefit from the additional workers. Allow them to settle.
We need no assistance from outsiders; we need only help ourselves! Dispatch a raiding party at once.

My lord! The monks speak of raging fires that cannot be quelled and believe this to be a sign sent by Kagutsuchi. They fear that the fire-god is displeased with our people and beseech you to appease him with a chinka-sai.
Such a military understanding will be a great asset to our cause. We accept.

Once more, the United States have offered to establish a military mission here to improve our military potential and improve trade relations. Should we decline again, I fear that they will not offer again.\\n\\nBy accepting we forego the opportunity to establish a military mission with other western powers.
Merchants operating at our ports have proposed increased trade between our clan and foreign nations. They have requested permission to invest in overseas trade opportunities.

Our forces have encountered a ronin band. Having lost their masters to the incessant warfare that has gripped the country, these yari samurai will fight for any daimyo willing to pay for their services. Left to their own devices, there is no telling what they might do.
We are grateful for this man's achievements; he shall be granted a fief for his efforts.
This distinguished warrior has secured many victories for our clan since assuming the mantle of general. Such accomplishments surely deserve recognition?
We cannot spare the time nor the necessary resources for dealing with these petty pirates. We will leave them be, for the time being at least.

A man has been found openly proselytising his Christian beliefs to the enlightened of this province. We can ill-afford the risk of religious schism should his teachings begin to influence the people of the clan.
We cannot accept. Convey our regrets to the British ambassador.
We must do justice to those whom this man has wronged. Bring me his head!
We have no need for his services.
My lord, a man with a passion for rangaku - learning born of our dealings with the Dutch - has been promoting western ideals and technology, believing them to be key to our national development.
Disturbing news, my lord. Twelve French seamen have been murdered in Sakai, near Osaka. It falls under our jurisdiction, and the French are demanding we pay damages.
We cannot afford to damage trade relations with the West for the sake of a few murderers. We will pay damages, see that the culprits are punished.
Such a military understanding will be a great asset to our cause. We accept.

We have been approached by the British. Staunch supporters of the Emperor, they have offered to establish a military mission here to improve our military potential and improve trade relations.\\n\\nBy accepting we forego the opportunity to establish a military mission with other western powers.
We need no assistance from outsiders; we need only help ourselves! Dispatch a raiding party at once.
The Miidera monks are valued friends. We will help fund their struggle.
We have no need to engage in trade with the nanban at this time. Perhaps at a later time?
They are right to be angry. Our constant dealings with the West has put our cultural identity in jeopardy. We must temper foreign relations.

My lord, we have received an envoy from the Miidera temple. Currently embroiled in a feud with their sister temple - Enryakuji - they have requested financial support and urge you to remember the assistance that they gave so willingly in the past.
The clan has benefited greatly from our relations with the nanban and our embrace of Christianity; the concerns of the people pale by comparison.
Improving the efficiency of industrial processes will benefit our economy. We should focus on western industrial science.
An army needs leadership if it is to wage war effectively. We will exercise his martial prowess to further our military objectives.
We cannot be held responsible for the consequences of foreign indiscretion, regardless of the severity.

Yamato province is home to numerous prominent temples. One such temple is the Todaiji; commanding considerable wealth and home to proficient warrior monks, their support could greatly aid our cause. Alternatively, sacking the temple may provide a quick source of funds.

Yamato province is home to numerous prominent temples. One such temple is the Kofukuji; commanding considerable wealth and home to proficient warrior monks, their support could greatly aid our cause. Alternatively, sacking the temple may provide a quick source of funds.
The clan's future is already assured; our son will come of age and he will lead our people admirably.
The happiness of our subects is paramount. We must better police the gaijin here; any attempts to resist will be dealt with severely.
Foreign trade necessitates foreign contact. Westerners and their ways have caused numerous 'incidents' at one of our trade ports and the people are concerned. What would you have us do?
We have no need to enlist the bandit remnants of fallen clans. Send them on their way.
This is an unfortunate situation, but we cannot be seen to interfere with the affairs of foreigners.

A Chinese man has appealed to us for aid. Having escaped from a nanban ship currently berthed in one of our harbours, he claims he and his companions were kidnapped for slavery and have been kept in appalling conditions.
We cannot spare the time nor the necessary resources for dealing with these petty pirates. We will leave them be, for the time being at least.

An emissary has been dispatched to us by the Ikko-Ikki monshu. In recognition of our devotion to Buddhist tradition and in return for some small compensation, the league is offering to support our monks in their work.
Our support for the nanban and their ways has made us strong. We will accept this honour.
Hoping to ease tensions within our domain, elders have requested that we invest in this years Sensosai in honour of our noble ancestors.
This man has worked hard and achieved much for his province and his clan. We acknowledge his efforts; he shall be rewarded.
Can Amaterasu be warning us of troubled times to come? We must remain cautious.
A prominent retainer in this province has expressed his discontent that the centralisation of power has diminished his authority in the region.

My lord, word has reached us that a rival clan has suffered following a terrible harvest. Their people are starving and their daimyo seeks aid from other clans. We may be at war, but does that mean that the toiling masses need suffer needlessly?
We need not invest further in the support of our ninja for the time being. We must decline the Hattori's offer.
The foreigners have caused us much turmoil of late and we will not have their misconceptions cloud the people's minds. He must be silenced!
We cannot stand idle while our people suffer. We shall fully occupy ourselves in the reconstruction effort, for the good of all.

Nanban traders have arrived at one of our ports, seeking permission to establish a trading post. Allowing this will give us access to new firearms. However, by doing so we run the risk of introducing undesirable nanban influences, such as Christianity, to the clan.
We cannot have the hearts and minds of our peasants corrupted by these antithetical beliefs. He must be executed at once.
You will remember mention of the Koyosha, my lord. Angered at our dealings with the West, these militant isolationists have taken to indiscriminately murdering foreign traders throughout our ports.

A man has been found openly speaking of his Buddhist beliefs amongst the good Christian people of this province. We can ill-afford the risk of religious schism should his teachings begin to influence the people of the clan.
The clan's best interests are paramount; we will allow him to keep the land and have it put to the best use - for a price.
The clan must have military superiority if we are to secure the shogunate. We will focus on military development.
A band of samurai, loyal to the Emperor and eager to serve, are seeking employment.
We have neither the time nor the money to squander on such superstitious frivolities.

Yamashiro province is home to numerous prominent temples. One such temple is the Miidera - commanding considerable wealth and home to proficient warrior monks, their support could greatly aid our cause. Alternatively, sacking the temple may provide a quick source of funds.
We can ill-afford to waste precious funds when so much is at stake.
This man’s ability is unquestionable, but we have no need of his services for the time being.
As you sit in a secluded shrine to Amaterasu, contemplating matters of Heaven and Earth, a three-legged crow perches upon a branch before you.
These upstarts should not presume to tamper with the balance of society. They must know their place; as such, their influence will be tempered.
We need no assistance from outsiders; we need only help ourselves! Dispatch a raiding party at once.
As the war effort escalates, an increasing number of drunken incidents are being reported as people seek solace in their cups from the many hardships it has brought.
An interesting, if frivolous notion. There is no need; our present actions alone serve to promote our greatness.
Once we have rid ourselves of the Minamoto scourge once and for all the tale can then be chronicled, but not before.
We have neither the time nor the money to squander on such superstitious frivolities.
An army needs leadership if it is to wage war effectively. We will give him the chance to prove his worth.
The people of this province are distraught at the behaviour of nanban missionaries in the area, who have desecrated sacred sites devoted to Buddha and the kami.

Despite the furore surrounding the opening of our ports to the West, we cannot deny the benefits of having access to their scientific knowledge.\\n\\nIn which field should we focus our research efforts?
We should work towards improving our navies.

Our forces have encountered a ronin band. Having lost their masters to the incessant warfare that has gripped the country, these katana samurai will fight for any daimyo willing to pay for their services. Left to their own devices, there is no telling what they might do.
The shogun's authority will enable our metsuke to dispense justice more effectively. We accept his offer.
Favourable weather conditions this year have led to an abundant harvest, so the clan has a surplus of agricultural produce.
We should work towards boosting production.

Your only male heir is young and inexperienced, my lord. Might I be so bold as to suggest adopting this worthy general, that he might lead the clan in your stead should some misfortune befall you before your son comes of age?
There are currently more pressing domestic issues needing our attention. Perhaps later?
We have no need to enlist the bandit remnants of fallen clans. Send them on their way.
Outrage! Do they not see how the people benefit from modernisation? These criminals must be hunted down!

A controversial request, my lord; our shirabyoshi seek to develop their repertoires through the Eiga Monogatari. The tale speaks highly of the Fujiwara, however, and its circulation will likely aid their cause.

You will remember mention of the Koyosha, my lord. Angered at our attempts to modernise at the expense of the samurai class, these militant traditionalists have taken to indiscriminately murdering clan and government officials.
A chance at improving our newly-recruited gunmen cannot be ignored; this man will be employed at once.

My lord, the Eiga Monogatari chronicles the exploits of Fujiwara Michinaga some hundred years past. By educating our shirabyoshi we improve their repertoire and foster greater interest in our own exploits.
Times of conflict are trying times indeed. The people must be allowed some leeway, as long as no criminal offence is committed.
A wise plan. We will invest in security at once.
There is an opportunity to send some of our subjects abroad to study under western scholars.
We need time to consider the options. Inform them that - for the time being at least - we decline.
A sound proposal; we can only benefit from further interaction with the Church. We will dispatch an embassy at once.

Merchants serving our clan in neighbouring provinces have discovered an opportunity to procure a quantity of military equipment and supplies. Investment in this could prove beneficial to our war efforts.
These additional supplies will allow us to effectively equip a considerable number of men. We will invest in this opportunity immediately.
We haven't the resources to spare at this time. Public order will have to be dealt with by other means.
We have no need for his services.
Hachiman sends blessings that our fishermen might better reap the teeming seas!
My lord, our men have apprehended a notorious brigand who is guilty of violence against our subjects. He is awaiting sentence.
A French ordnance expert going by the name of Clateau is offering - for a price - to help improve our use of artillery.

My lord, we have received an envoy from the Enryakuji temple. Currently embroiled in a feud with their sister temple - Miidera - they have requested financial support and urge you to remember the assistance that they gave so willingly in the past.
Such a military understanding will be a great asset to our cause. We accept.
We will focus on attack so that we will have clear advantage over our enemies when pressing the offensive.
My lord! The monks speak of dark skies closing in above our lands and believe this to be an omen sent by Fujin. They fear a coming storm and beseech you to honour the wind-god.

Nanban traders have arrived at one of our ports, seeking permission to establish a trading post. Allowing this will give us access to new firearms. However, by doing so we run the risk of introducing undesirable nanban influences, such as Christianity, to the clan.

Nanban traders have arrived at one of our ports, seeking permission to establish a trading post. Allowing this will give us access to new firearms. However, by doing so we run the risk of introducing undesirable nanban influences, such as Christianity, to the clan.
With so many provinces under our control, we have dominion over an increasing number of jizamurai. These minor land-holders are both excellent warriors and agricultural experts.

We have been approached by the United States. They have offered to establish a military mission here to improve our military potential and improve trade relations. \\n\\nBy accepting we forego the opportunity to establish a military mission with other western powers.
In this time of conflict, our strategists are divided as to whether to invest the clan's military resources in the practice of either offensive or defensive techniques.
The chance of further improving our archers cannot be ignored; this man will be employed at once.

A landowner in this province has seized estates belonging to his neighbour. The victim demands justice, while the offending party claims that he will make better use of the soil for the good of the clan.
With the fervent support of the Ikko-Ikki, our monks will be better able to disseminate the Buddha's teachings amongst the people. We accept the league's offer.
By concentrating on firearms training, we can ensure that we have the upper hand in battle.
The tale is not yet ended. Once our family stands victorious over the Taira…that will be an epic tale!
My lord! Our support for their religion has strengthened the ties between the clan and our Christian friends. As such, they have expressed their wish to have you baptised in their faith.
Wars are to be won with spears and swords, not with rice. We will send aid to our noble foe and his people.
This province has recently been ravaged by the forces of nature. Many of our people have had their livelihoods ruined. The process of rebuilding all that was lost is an arduous affair.
It is a good thing that, despite the hardships of our time, our clansmen can find solace in beauty.
The chance of training finer swordsmen cannot be ignored; this man will be employed at once.
Economic growth will be stifled as long as only a select few are allowed to hold the reins. Their power will be curbed.
They will have their chance to serve the Emperor, employ them at once.
Inari's kitsune has come to bless our crops; a time of prosperity is at hand!
Inari's kitsune has come to bless our crops; a time of prosperity is at hand!
By approaching them at this time we risk deterioration of important long-term relations with the West.
Amaterasu approves of our clan's great undertakings! Yatagarasu has come to lead us to victory!
Our ability to master the arts is beyond question. We see no reason to pursue this course at this time.
The nanban missionary Francis Xavier, having treated with other daimyo to propagate his Christian teachings in their domains, seeks permission for his missionaries to preach in ours.
This man must not be allowed to poison the people's spirits and sully ancient traditions with his false nanban teachings.
We need no assistance from outsiders; we need only help ourselves! Dispatch a raiding party at once.
Conflict has escalated, and our domain has grown strong serving the Shogun. A decision must now be made: how best to unify the people of Japan once and for all?
We have indeed been blessed! No mouth will go unfed this year;  the surplus will be distributed amongst the peasants.
Despite the furore surrounding the opening of our ports to the West, we cannot deny the benefits of having access to their knowledge of economics.\\n\\nIn which area should we focus our research?
We have no need for any matchlocks at this time.
An army needs leadership if it is to wage war effectively. We will give him the chance to prove his worth.
Diligence is insufficient. It is their duty to push themselves to their limits for the good of the clan.
The leader of a warrior band from this province seeks an audience, my lord. He and his men are distraught at the court's increasing levies upon the bushidan.

A renowned horseman has approached the clan, enticed by our reputation for optimum use of cavalry in the field. For a price, this master of horse and mounted combat has offered to help improve the skills of our cavalrymen.
A chance to field stronger cavalry cannot be ignored; this man will be employed at once.
We will give him a chance to prove his loyalty to the clan and to preserve his honour by committing seppuku at once.

I must speak with you, my lord. The unequal trade agreements established with the western powers have greatly harmed our economy; the people toil diligently, yet they receive little compensation. Is there nothing we can do?
A gaijin claiming to be a master of matchlock weaponry is offering - for a price - to instruct our clan's warriors in the use of firearms.
Our war effort will benefit from a better understanding of military engineering.
The Emperor has commissioned the construction of a grand new palace and has requested contributions from all the great clans towards this glorious undertaking.
Impressed with our efforts to support Christianity across Japan, the Church has offered to grant us powers on behalf of the Pope himself, in return for tribute.
Such a military understanding will be a great asset to our cause. We accept.
Spring has come. The people have toiled throughout the winter to ensure that our coffers remain filled, reinforcing the strength of the clan.
We have neither the time nor the money to squander on such superstitious frivolities.
The awkward customs of the nanban have led to an increased number of undesirable and regrettable incidents in this province.
My lord, the Jesuit Alessandro Valignano has brought us a proposal. He believes we should send an embassy to the Pope. This is surely an opportunity to strengthen our relations with the Church.
Unrest is a small price to pay for the benefits of trade with the nanban. The people must deal with the matter themselves.
Our time will not be wasted on such frivolities.
There will always be peasants to till the land. Our clan will benefit from the increase in trade brought about by such migration.
It is impressive that this youth managed to inflitrate us beneath the guards' very noses! Release him to his family, and allow him as many supplies as he can carry.

As trade opportunities grow in this province, merchants and artisans have established trade guilds to secure monopolies over their trades. Independent artisans and traders are being put out of business.
We will obtain written apologies from those involved; no harm was done, and we will not have our people submit to western justice.
We will arrange a clan-wide Fujin-sai at once; see to it that no expense is spared in the preparation of the appropriate rites and festivities.
We welcome the opportunity to engage in material and cultural exchange with the nanban. We will build them a trading post.
We welcome the opportunity to engage in material and cultural exchange with the nanban. We will build them a trading post.
Their expertise in agrarian matters makes them invaluable to the economy; they will best serve their clan managing the land.

A reputable marksman has approached the clan, offering his services so that we might extend our reputation for skill with the bow. This man's mastery of ranged fighting knows no equal; for a price, he has offered to improve the proficiency of our archers.
We are not prepared to adopt this man into our family. Heirs will be forthcoming.
In honour of His Majesty and of his divine authority, we will send offerings for this year's choga.
My lord, an increasing number of labourers from neighbouring provinces are seeking to settle in your domain, hoping to escape the hardships imposed by their lords.
The needs of the clan far outweigh those of the emperor. We will give no tribute.
Despite the furore surrounding the opening of our ports to the West, we cannot deny the benefits of having access to their knowledge of engineering.\\n\\nIn which field should we focus our research?
We have no need to further invest in the support of our missionaries for the time being. We decline the Church's offer.
Enough of this! Never again will we submit to these so-called rulers. We must take the future into our own hands.
These cannon will help topple our enemies’ defences. We will take them.
We have neither the time nor the money to squander on such superstitious frivolities.
We will arrange a clan-wide chinka-sai at once; see to it that no expense is spared in the preparation of the rites and the festivities.
Increased trade with prosperous nations will in turn boost our clan's economic fortunes. The merchants will be given the proper concessions.
Despite tensions, conflict has yet to escalate; there may never be a better time to develop our infrastructure. We will seek the counsel of experts in civil technology.
We have neither the time nor the money to squander on such superstitious frivolities.
He will be kept alive to make amends for his repeated failure. The dishonour will be his punishment.
A samurai cannot serve the clan if he is dead. He will be allowed to atone for his misdeeds through service to his lord.
We cannot allow the hearts and minds of our peasants corrupted by these antithetical beliefs. He must be executed at once.

A noble in your service will be throwing a lavish feast in the family's honour within the month. He knows you are burdened by duty, but hopes that you will in some way be able to participate in the event.
We must have military superiority if we are to prevail over our enemies. We will focus on military development.
We have neither the time nor the money to squander on such superstitious frivolities.
My lord, a group of westerners - guests within our realm - were attacked in the night by known Imperialist elements. Nobody was harmed, but great offence has been taken.
They will have their chance to serve the Shogun; employ them at once.
Kondo Isami, head of the shinsengumi - staunch enforcers of the Shogun's will - is seeking to strengthen his ranks. He is appealing to Shogunate loyalists to assist in his recruitment drive.
We will do whatever it takes to protect the Shogunate's interests. Let us assist him in his mission.
Hachiman sends blessings that our fishermen might better reap the teeming seas!
Now, more than ever, we rely on guns to achieve victory on the battlefield. Both firearms and artillery have a major role to play in warfare, and we should exploit them to suit our military ends.
Whilst surveying our domain, an astonishing sight was observed outside a shrine to Inari; a nine-tailed fox, sitting sentinel-like beneath a torii.
Whilst surveying our domain, an astonishing sight was observed outside a shrine to Inari; a nine-tailed fox, sitting sentinel-like beneath a torii.
In troubled times such as these it is the right of all to bear arms in defence, to say nothing of tradition. We will take no such action.
The Shogun has demanded that, as a mark of dedication and of respect towards his supreme power, all great clans should pay tribute to the Shogunate.
My lord, one of our nobles speaks most favourably about a shirabyoshi who has recently entered into his service; by all accounts she is the most enchanting lady ever to have graced our lands.
We are currently in no position to draw the ire of Kyoto. We will acquiesce to the shogun's request - for now.
Conflict has escalated, and we have grown strong serving the Emperor. A decision must now be made: how best to unify the people of Japan once and for all?

My lord, one of our valiant warriors has proved his mettle in securing this victory for the clan. It is our belief that his talents are wasted in the rank-and-file and that he will best serve the clan leading our forces in battle.
Enough of this! Never again will we submit to these so-called rulers. Let us take our people's future into our own hands.

My lord, you are without a male heir. Should some misfortune befall you our great clan would be doomed to fall. This man has proved himself capable of great deeds; adopting him as your son will secure the clan's future whilst duly honouring his diligence.
My lord, we should take advantage of our good relations with the western power, and use Jesuit advisors to help further our knowledge and skills.
They owe the court nothing; they are our subjects to tax as we see fit.

Owing to our progressive trade policies, a Jesuit missionary within our nanban quarter has managed to broker an agreement between our clan and his masters. In return, he offers some fine weapons as a gift.
Unfortunately, we haven't the means to assist the shinsengumi at the moment.
Construction and development will benefit from a better understanding of civil engineering.
My lord! The monks speak of dark skies closing in above our lands and believe this to be an omen sent by Fujin. They fear a coming storm and beseech you to honour the wind-god.
Such a military understanding will be a great asset to our cause. We accept.
A flight of doves has been seen circling the capital, my lord. Hachiman's flock brings divine blessings upon our clan!
Hachiman favours our war efforts and grants our warriors the blessings of Heaven!
We will address this issue, lest our lucrative - if unsanctioned - trade deals suffer at the hands of the pirates.
Who are we, to be cowed by this petty charlatan? Before long, he will be brought to his knees before the might of our clan.
How is it that this thief, barely a man, gained access to our stores? Throw him back on the street and bring me the guard captain; dereliction of duty will not be tolerated!
Nothing good may be said for merchants and tinkerers; it is the hard work of the farmer class on which the clan thrives.

Research is underway and we have been approached by numerous foreigners claiming to be experts in their field. We can exploit this to our advantage; should we seek to focus on civil or military development?
Duty calls and time is precious, but we will honour his gesture towards the family with a modest contribution.
A band of samurai, loyal to the Shogun and eager to serve, are seeking employment.
Seeking to further the clan's mastery of the arts our retainers request guidance as to where they should focus their efforts.
We will capture those responsible and hand them over to the westerners for punishment.
We have little use for another lord's disgruntled peasantry: satisfying our own people's needs is trying enough. Send them on their way.
This conflict has yet to escalate, but all-out war is inevitable. When that day comes we must have the advantage. We will seek the counsel of experts in military technology.
We must ensure that good trade relations with Korea are maintained. We will deal with the pirates at once.
Artillery allows us to strike our enemies from afar, leaving them vulnerable once we have closed in.
We cannot afford to damage trade relations with the West for the sake of a few murderers. We will pay damages and see that the culprits are punished.
My lord! The monks speak of a gathering of great turtles upon our shores and believe this to be a sign sent by Owatatsumi. They fear ominous tides and beseech you to honour the sea-god.
We will arrange shiomatsuri in all provinces that sit upon the Owatatsumi's waters. See to it that no expense is spared in the preparations of the rites and the festivities.
Inari favours our martial efforts and grants our warriors the blessings of heaven!
Inari favours our martial efforts and grants our warriors the blessings of heaven!
We currently have no need for their services; they will have to seek their fortunes elsewhere.
Her charms could serve to inspire our generals or manipulate our enemies. Employ her at once.
This man’s ability is unquestionable, but we have no need of his services for the time being.
My lord! The monks speak of a rumbling deep beneath the mountains and believe this to be a sign sent by Oyamatsumi. They fear the wrath of the earth and beseech you to appease the mountain-god.
His continued violation of our clan's reputation ends here. Have him commit seppuku at once.
Honour and tradition must be maintained. He is to commit seppuku at once.
Deference will be shown to the great kami of the mountains; see to it that no expense is spared in the preparation of the appropriate rites and festivities.
We must assure the future of our clan, and this man has proved his dedication to our cause. We will adopt him as heir.
A disgrace to the clan, this man is not worthy to lead our armies in battle. His death is the only means by which the shame and dishonour he has brought upon the clan can be undone.

One of your subjects has been disgraced after repeatedly failing to serve the clan to the best of his ability on the battlefield. As a matter of honour, he beseeches you to allow his immediate suicide.

These are dark times, my lord. Murder abounds: murder of foreigners; murder over petty matters of class; murder for political ideals. It may be necessary to forbid the carrying of weapons amongst the people.
The Emperor must lead his people to greatness. In his name, we will crush all who oppose us!
Western knowledge will keep us one step ahead of our rivals: send word to the nanban quarter at once!
These fine matchlocks are certainly a worthy addition to our arsenal. We will see to it that our troops receive the guns at once.
We should investigate improved methods of distribution.
An army needs leadership if it is to wage war effectively. We will exercise his martial prowess to further our military objectives.
Our inclination towards the use of close quarters fighters in battle has led to a master of kenjutsu approaching the clan and offering to improve our infantry's swordsmanship - for a price.

Yamashiro province is home to numerous prominent temples. One such temple is the Enryakuji; commanding considerable wealth and home to proficient warrior monks, their support could greatly aid our cause. Alternatively, sacking the temple may provide a quick source of funds.
He may yet be of some use to us, so will be given the chance to redeem himself on this occasion.
It is too early to tell. In due course we shall see where his true loyalties lie; for now we will make use of his talents.
Such behaviour cannot go unpunished. We will expel the offending gaijin and take steps to ensure that this does not happen again.
We cannot accept. Convey our regrets to the French ambassador.
The Shogun must lead his people to greatness. In his name, we will crush all those who oppose us!
Amaterasu approves of our clan's great undertakings! Yatagarasu has come to lead us to victory!
There are more pressing issues to attend to than the trivial problems faced by the peasantry.

My lord, the son of one of your most loyal subjects - a fine warrior, I am told -  desires to prove his worth to you and the clan. As such, he requests the opportunity to lead your glorious forces in battle.
We have profited from the nanban, but a line must be drawn at such disregard for tradition and our ancestors.
A scholar seeks your endorsement, hoping to chronicle the events of the Heiji Rebellion, when Lord Yoshitomo sought to oust the corrupt cloistered Emperor but fell foul of Taira schemes.
They owe the court nothing; they reside within our domain and their service to the clan is payment enough. We will see to it that they are no longer troubled by the court.

Once more, the French have offered to establish a military mission here to improve our military potential and improve trade relations. Should we decline again, I fear that they will not offer again.\\n\\nBy accepting we forego the opportunity to establish a military mission with other western powers.
This is a sad state of affairs. We will appeal to the westerners to improve the situation.
He demands too much for his services; our own sensei are more than adequate for the training of archers.
We need all the help we can get in this conflict. Engage him, may his skills help keep our men alive.
If rumours regarding this man's loyalties are to be believed he is nothing but a traitor to the clan. Action must be taken, lest he be allowed to bring further shame upon or cause harm to the people.
Rumours abound regarding this man's waning loyalty to us. We must be wary of such sedition lest it propagates and compromises the clan.
The Pope's divine authority will allow the word of God to be spread more effectively. We accept the Church's offer.
The glory of Amaterasu's lineage must be honoured. See to it that the Emperor receives the tribute that he requires.
This man’s eagerness to serve is admirable, but we do not require his services for the time being.
Such a military understanding will be a great asset to our cause. We accept.
In honour of their efforts and of the flowering of the cherry blossom, we will arrange festivities for the people.
An interesting notion, but our resources are best directed at Japanese matters.
The benefits of improved relations with the nanban, and, by extension, an increase of trade in goods and knowledge, cannot be ignored.

My lord! The monks speak of raging fires that cannot be quelled and believe this to be a sign sent by Kagutsuchi. They fear that the fire-god is displeased with our people and beseech you to appease him with a chinka-sai.
The uncertainty of the harvest and the increasing profitability of the life of the artisan is driving people away from the land and into the towns.
A young man has been apprehended by the guards, my lord. He was caught stealing supplies from the quartermaster's stores; claiming that he sought only to provide for his impoverished family.
He demands too much for his services; our own sensei are more than capable of training our swordsmen.
A fair price. Buy their stock and have them delivered to the dojo at once.
We have no need for this gaijin's extortionate services. Our men need only aim and fire; mastery will surely follow in time.
A nanban ship has been wrecked nearby. The survivors, seeking to buy safe passage home, are selling salvage. Among the items are matchlock guns, sufficient to equip a unit of samurai.
As long as we are paid our dues, these trade guilds may continue as they please.
He demands too much for his services; our own sensei are more than adequate for our cavalry training needs.
What good is military might without the infrastructure to support and exploit it? We will focus on administrative development.
